sections.title: RESO Web API Technical Standards
sections.contentHtml:
The technical architecture of the U.S. real estate market is now built exclusively on the RESO Web API standard. The legacy Real Estate Transaction Standard (RETS) is fully deprecated and must not be used in any 2026 integration.

Data Dictionary 2.0 Certification

NAR-affiliated MLSs were required to achieve RESO Data Dictionary 2.0 certification by April 2025. Significant updates include standardized status values (Active, Pending, Closed), detailed environmental hazard fields (fire/flood risk), and transition to 'Primary' terminology for property features.

UPCOMING: RESO is moving toward Data Dictionary 2.1 and 2.2 releases, which introduce fields for digital staging and AI-enhanced photo disclosure including MediaDigitalEnhancementYN (see Section 11).

Deprecated and New Fields — 2026

FieldStatus
Action RequiredBuyerAgencyCompensation
DEPRECATED — Removed post-settlementRemove from ingestion schema; treat as null.
AutomatedValuationModelYNActive — Correct spelling
Respect opt-out flag per property.MediaDigitalEnhancementYN
New in DD 2.2Flag AI-staged photos on display; show side-by-side with original.
OriginalMediaUrlNew / Updated
Provides non-altered source images; required by emerging consumer protection laws.ListingKeyNumeric
ActiveUse as primary unique identifier for listings across feeds.

TLS 1.3 strongly recommended.Auth Method

RETS — PROHIBITEDReject any MLS still on RETS; flag for escalation.
